title: Entryway Bench and Storage Shelf with Hooks
summary: A two-piece mudroom set — a cubby bench to sit and stash shoes, plus a matching hook-and-shelf that mounts above it. Built from 1-by boards.
description:
This is the classic drop-zone combo: a bench with open cubbies for shoes and bags below, and a wall-mounted shelf with hooks above for coats and keys. Building them as two pieces is smart — the bench is structural and gets screwed together, while the shelf is light and can be brad-nailed, and mounting them separately lets you set the hook height to whoever actually uses it.

The bench is built from 1x12s, which the source notes honestly come out shallow — fine for a mudroom perch, but if you want a roomier seat, swap every 1x12 for a wider board (a 16" project panel) and keep the same layout. Because people sit on it, join the main boards with screws or pocket holes, not just nails.

The two details that make it look finished: cut the shelf's side pieces at an angle at the top so there's no sharp corner at eye level, and mount the shelf by screwing through the thick 1x8 hook board into a wall stud — a shelf loaded with heavy winter coats will pull anchors out of drywall. Find the stud.

materials.name: 1x12, 8 ft
materials.note: Bench top and bottom. Swap for a 16" project panel for a deeper seat.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, 48" x 24"
materials.note: Backs for both the bench and the shelf.

cutList.note: Cut top at an angle to avoid a sharp corner at eye level.

steps.title: Build the bench box
steps.body: Because people sit on it, join the main 1x12 boards with 2" screws or 1-1/4" pocket screws (3/4" setting) rather than nails alone. Attach the sides to the bottom, then set the dividers to create the cubbies.

steps.title: Add the seat and trim
steps.body: Attach the seat top to the base with 2" screws or pocket holes. Measure and cut the 1x4 trim to fit — top sides, footer sides, and footer front — and attach all with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. If the center footer is hard to attach to the bottom, add a scrap 1x4 block flat-nailed to the underside as a backer.

steps.title: Back the bench
steps.body: Cut the bench back with a circular saw and save the scrap for the shelf back. Attach with 3/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build the shelf box
steps.body: Build the shelf with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails — it isn't structural. Assemble the main shelf and back with the dividers. Cut the side pieces' tops at an angle so there's no sharp corner at eye level, and attach them.

steps.title: Finish the shelf and add the top
steps.body: Attach the shelf top back (1x2) and top front (1x8) with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Then attach the 1/4" back with 3/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Mount hooks and hang, then finish
steps.body: Attach the double hooks to the thick hook board. To hang the shelf, locate a wall stud and screw through the 1x8 hook board into the stud — a shelf full of heavy coats will pull drywall anchors out. Sand and finish both pieces to match.

images.alt: An entryway set with a cubby bench below and a hook-and-shelf coat organizer above
